focus: AI-governed reviewer independence without human code-detail review

blocking_findings.summary: The undefined next target threshold shortcut could allow tiny reductions to count as terminal progress.
The initial plan did not operationalize independent review strongly enough to prevent implementation self-approval.
The extracted-module 400/500 line threshold could block legitimate large-file refactors or force harmful micro-shards.

non_blocking_findings: Path-renaming bypass needed explicit filesystem scanning and similarity follow-up.
Near-threshold file accumulation needed a secondary budget.
Moving branch baselines could allow two-commit inflation.
Accepted files over 750 lines needed enforceable re-challenge.

non_blocking_findings.summary: Plan statuses needed to be updated after delta review.
The operational meaning of a separate reviewer invocation should be clarified during LFA-P1.8.
The 800-line extracted-module ceiling may need monitoring during the first strict test-shard split.

resolution.summary: The plan now defines numeric threshold floors plus a 15% minimum reduction, replaces the overstrict extracted-module threshold with an 800-line domain-aware ceiling, requires tracked filesystem debt scanning, adds near-threshold and accepted-file re-challenge gates, and clarifies reviewer anti-self-dealing requirements.
